intro: Lycianthes rantonnetii (also known as Lycianthes somniculenta) is a plant species in the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the "night-scented flower" or "scented fig-marigold", which produces fragrant flowers that bloom at night and release their scent in the evening. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. The bioactive constituents of Lycianthes rantonnetii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep patterns.
